data.title: Sanofi strikes autoimmune drug deal for up to $1.9B
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) has agreed to acquire an immunology drug from clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company Dren Bio for up to $1.9B.

The French pharmaceutical company will acquire DR-0201, a CD20-directed bispecific antibody currently being evaluated in a Phase 1 trial in B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma patients and is expanding into various autoimmune indications.

The drug candidate has shown a favorable preclinical safety profile in multiple non-human primate studies, with no cytokine release syndrome or neurotoxicity.

Under the terms of the merger agreement, Sanofi will acquire DR-0201 through the acquisition of the Dren Bio affiliate Dren-0201 for an upfront payment of $600M and potential future payments totaling $1.3B upon achievement of certain development and launch milestones.

The companies expect the transaction to close during the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: Enerflex CEO Marc Rossiter steps down; appoints Preet Dhindsa as interim CEO and reaffirms FY25 outlook
European markets mixed as traders await central bank verdicts
Alvotech acquires Xbrane’s R&D operations in Sweden and biosimilar candidate XB003
data.description:
Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) Thursday announced the acquisition of Xbrane Biopharma AB’s R&D operations and a biosimilar candidate, establishing a footprint in the Swedish life science sector.

The acquisition’s purchase price equals a value of about SEK 275 million ($27 million) and will be payable as SEK 102.2 million in cash at closing and by assumption of SEK 172.8 million in debt and accounts payable.

The acquisition includes Xbrane’s R&D operations based in Campus Solna, at the Karolinska Institute outside Stockholm, Sweden, as well as the biosimilar candidate XB003, referencing Cimzia.

Xbrane retains other pre-clinical assets and will focus on the commercialization of this portfolio, Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) said, adding that it also intends to explore the possibility of a listing of Swedish Depository Receipts, equity share equivalents, on Nasdaq Stockholm, in the future.

The creditors have agreed to accept payment for SEK 152.8 million of the debt with Alvotech equity shares.

data.title: Trump calls on Fed to cut rates as tariffs start to 'ease' into economy
data.description:
President Donald Trump said the Federal Reserve should cut interest rates as tariffs start to "ease" into the economy, after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said tariffs may delay further progress in bringing inflation to the 2% target.

"The Fed would be MUCH better off CUTTING RATES as U.S. tariffs start to transition (ease!) their way into the economy," Trump posted on his social media app Truth Social. "Do the right thing. April 2nd is Liberation Day in America!!!"

April 2 is when Trump will unveil new reciprocal tariffs on various countries based on the rates they charge for American goods. The scope of the new tariffs remains unclear.

The Fed on Wednesday kept its policy interest rate unchanged at 4.25%-4.50%, as expected. Fed officials still expect two 25-basis-point rate cuts this year, but forecast slower economic growth and higher inflation.

In his post-decision presser, Powell said the tariff effect should be "transitory," and long-term inflation expectations are fairly stable overall. But he acknowledged the high level of uncertainty in the economy. 

"Staying in wait-and-see mode is viewed as a reasonable compromise," said Kathy Jones, chief fixed income strategist at Schwab Center for Financial Research. "Given the cross currents facing the economy, we do not expect a change in interest rate policy from the Fed until later this year, if at all."

data.title: E3 Lithium finalizes $4.4 million funding agreement
data.description:
• E3 Lithium (OTCQX:EEMMF) Thursday said that it has executed the contribution agreement for $4.40 million in non-repayable funding from the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund.
  • Under the agreement terms, E3 Lithium will receive reimbursement for 50% of eligible expenditures up to the total $4.4 million allocation.
  • The investment will be used to undertake preconstruction work on the necessary transportation and energy infrastructure to develop the Clearwater Project.
  • E3 previously announced on March 3, 2025, conditional approval for up to $4.7 million in funding from CMIF.

data.title: Trump expects India to cut tariffs, but threatens to impose reciprocal duties from April 2
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said India will be hit by reciprocal tariffs from April 2, even as officials in New Delhi look to appease Washington by reducing trade barriers.

Trump on Wednesday told Breitbart News that he believes that India will probably be lowering the tariffs it imposes on American goods while adding that the U.S. administration will impose the same charges on India, starting April 2.

“I believe they’re probably going to be lowering those tariffs substantially, but on April 2, we will be charging them the same tariffs they charge us,” Trump said.

According to a statement by the Ministry of External Affairs, India is looking to reduce tariff and non-tariff barriers with the U.S. and strengthen trade in the goods and services sector through the multi-sector Bilateral Trade Agreement that is under the works.

However, India, the world’s second-biggest steel producer, is looking to impose duties of 12% on most steel imports, in-line with measures taken by other nations and comes after the U.S. imposed tariffs.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: British unemployment rate unchanged at 4.4% in January
data.description:
• The United Kingdom’s unemployment rate held steady at 4.4% from November to January 2025, in line with expectations, and remained at its highest level since the three months ending in May last year.
  • The number of individuals unemployed for up to 12 months rose, while those unemployed for over 12 months decreased.
  • Compared to the same period last year, the number of people unemployed for both up to 12 months and over 12 months increased. 

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: German PPI rises 0.70% in February, less than expected
data.description:
• Producer Prices in Germany increased 0.70% in February over the same month in the previous year. 

  • On a month-on-month basis, German PPI decreased to 0.2% in February from 0.1% in January.

data.title: Asia markets mixed after Fed hold; China holds LPR rate steady for 5th month
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets mixed on Thursday, tracking an upbeat session on Wall Street overnight after the Fed kept interest rates steady while signaling two more rate cuts for 2025 but lowered GDP forecasts and raised inflation projections. Investors also digested the latest monetary policy decision from the People’s Bank of China.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) traded around $86,000 on Thursday, hitting its highest levels in nearly two weeks during the session, as optimism grew ahead of US President Donald Trump’s speech at Blockwork’s Digital Asset Summit later today.

Meanwhile, tensions in the Middle East escalated as Israel launched “targeted ground activities” in Gaza, retaking part of a key area after an airstrike ended a two-month ceasefire with Hamas. 

Japan (NKY:IND) market closed. The Japanese yen appreciated past 148.5 per dollar on Thursday, rising for the second straight session

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.46% to 3,420, while the Shenzhen Component dropped 0.3% to 10,940 on Thursday, extending losses from the previous session as the People’s Bank of China left key lending rates unchanged, in line with expectations, and the offshore yuan edged lower to around 7.23 per dollar.

China's youth unemployment rate climbed to 16.9% in February 2025, up from 16.1% in the previous month, according to data from the National Bureau of Statistics released on Thursday.

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 1.87% to 24,535 in Thursday morning deals, ending gains from the prior two sessions amid widespread sector declines. 

The Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) maintained its base rate unchanged at 4.75% on March 20, hours after the US Fed held steady its borrowing costs, as widely expected, following the second FOMC meeting of the year.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.62% to 75,770 in early deals on Thursday, mainly boosted by gains from the media, tech sectors, and auto.

Australia (AS51) rose 1.16% to close at 7,919 on Thursday, rebounding from the previous session’s losses. The Australian dollar fell below $0.635 on Thursday, marking its third straight decline, as traders reassessed the Reserve Bank of Australia’s monetary policy outlook following weaker-than-expected jobs data. 

In the U.S., on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher following the Federal Reserve's decision to keep rates steady, as anticipated, while signaling two more rate cuts later this year. 

U.S. stock futures climbed on Thursday after the Federal Reserve reaffirmed its outlook for two interest rate cuts this year: Dow +0.32%; S&P 500 +0.43%; Nasdaq +0.55%.

Investors now await weekly jobless claims data for further labor market insights. Earnings reports from Nike, FedEx, Micron Technology, and Darden Restaurants are also on the radar.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • People’s Bank of China keeps lending rates unchanged, in line with expectations

  • Australia's Feb unemployment rate remains unchanged at 4.1%, aligning with expectations

  • Japan’s trade balance swung to surplus in February, driven by strong exports
  • Bank of Japan keeps interest rates unchanged at 0.5%, as widely expected
  • China’s retail sales accelerate 4.0% Y/Y; industrial output grow 5.9% y/y exceeds forecasts; unveils special action plan
